attacking_style: Pure sprinter relying on explosive final acceleration from 200-300m, most effective from third wheel or when launched early (800m+) by surprise timing rather than classic power leadouts.
team_tactics: Quick-Step deploys early surprise leadouts (800m+) via MØRKØV to catch rivals malpositioned, rather than sustained power trains. Team abandons support on lumpy terrain, prioritizing GC riders over sprint.
terrain_preference: Flat bunch sprints with straightforward finales. Exposed and dropped on moderate second-category climbs and lumpy stages; team withdraws support when terrain undulates.
vulnerability: Poor positioning instincts in chaotic finales (boxed at 1600m, abandoned leadout wheel). Loses team commitment on any lumpy terrain. Struggles when forced to initiate own sprint without setup.
key_patterns: MØRKØV executes early surprise leadouts (800m) to neutralize rivals through positioning, not power (3 races)
Dropped or unsupported on any lumpy/climbing stages; pure sprinter bandwidth exposed (3 races)
Positioning errors in chaos: boxed badly, abandoned team wheels, left-side acceleration mistakes (3 races)
Explosive speed from third wheel compensates for positioning weaknesses when setup works (2 races)
Team deprioritizes him for GC riders (ALAPHILIPPE, GILBERT) when terrain suits climbers (2 races)
form_trajectory: negative: 2021 showed no systematic leadout support and only seventh place, significant decline from 2018-2019 wins when team fully committed
